index.title: C++ Tutorial - Complete Guide from Basics to Advanced with Practice
index.description: Learn C++ from scratch with our free, detailed chapter-by-chapter guide. Covers fundamentals, OOP, STL, memory management, and competitive programming. Each topic includes detailed explanations, code examples, and 50+ practice questions.
index.keywords: c++ tutorial, learn c++, c++ for beginners, c++ programming guide, c++ stl tutorial, c++ oops, c++ competitive programming, c++ interview questions, c++ practice questions, c++ data structures
index.intro:
C++ is one of the most powerful and widely used programming languages in the world. From operating systems and game engines to competitive programming and high-frequency trading systems, C++ is everywhere performance matters. If you are a college student preparing for placements, competitive programming, or system-level development, C++ is essential.

This guide takes you from writing your first Hello World to mastering advanced topics like STL, memory management, templates, and competitive programming patterns. Every chapter includes detailed explanations of what each concept is and why it matters, followed by real code you can compile and run.

Each chapter has 50+ practice questions ranging from easy warm-ups to hard interview-level problems. By the end of this guide, you will be confident writing efficient C++ code for any purpose.

chapters.title: Introduction to C++
chapters.description: Learn what C++ is, its history, where it is used, and how it compares to other languages like Java and Python.

chapters.title: Setting Up C++ and Your First Program
chapters.description: Install a C++ compiler, set up an IDE, and write your first program with cout, cin, and the main function.

chapters.title: Variables, Data Types, and Type Casting
chapters.description: Understand int, float, double, char, bool, sizeof, type casting, auto keyword, and constants.

chapters.title: Operators in C++
chapters.description: Master arithmetic, relational, logical, bitwise, assignment, and ternary operators with precedence rules.

chapters.title: Input, Output, and Formatting
chapters.description: Use cin, cout, getline, setw, setprecision, fixed, and learn about buffer flushing and input validation.

chapters.title: Conditional Statements (if, else, switch)
chapters.description: Make decisions with if-else, nested conditions, ternary operator, and switch-case statements.

chapters.title: Loops (for, while, do-while) and Patterns
chapters.description: Master all loop types, nested loops, break/continue, and build star, number, and character patterns.

chapters.title: Functions, Overloading, and Recursion
chapters.description: Define functions, understand pass-by-value vs pass-by-reference, function overloading, default arguments, and recursion.

chapters.title: Arrays and Multidimensional Arrays
chapters.description: Work with 1D and 2D arrays, array operations, passing arrays to functions, and common array algorithms.

chapters.title: Strings - C-style and std::string
chapters.description: Master C-strings, std::string methods, string manipulation, comparison, and common string algorithms.

chapters.title: Pointers and References
chapters.description: Understand memory addresses, pointer arithmetic, references, pointer to pointer, and common pitfalls.

chapters.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation (new, delete)
chapters.description: Allocate memory at runtime with new/delete, dynamic arrays, memory leaks, and smart pointers introduction.

chapters.title: OOP - Classes, Objects, and Constructors
chapters.description: Create classes with data members, member functions, constructors, destructors, this pointer, and static members.

chapters.title: Encapsulation, Access Specifiers, and Friend Functions
chapters.description: Use public, private, protected access, getters/setters, friend functions, and friend classes.

chapters.title: Inheritance in C++
chapters.description: Implement single, multiple, multilevel, and hierarchical inheritance with access control and constructor chaining.

chapters.title: Polymorphism - Virtual Functions and Operator Overloading
chapters.description: Master compile-time (overloading) and runtime (virtual functions, vtable) polymorphism and operator overloading.

chapters.title: Abstract Classes, Pure Virtual Functions, and Interfaces
chapters.description: Design with abstract classes, pure virtual functions, and interface patterns in C++.

chapters.title: Templates - Function and Class Templates
chapters.description: Write generic code with function templates, class templates, template specialization, and variadic templates.

chapters.title: Exception Handling (try, catch, throw)
chapters.description: Handle errors with try-catch, throw, custom exceptions, exception hierarchy, and noexcept.

chapters.title: STL Containers - vector, list, map, set, stack, queue
chapters.description: Master the Standard Template Library containers with their methods, time complexities, and use cases.

chapters.title: STL Algorithms and Iterators
chapters.description: Use sort, find, binary_search, accumulate, transform, and iterators to write efficient C++ code.

chapters.title: File Handling (ifstream, ofstream, fstream)
chapters.description: Read and write files, handle binary files, and implement file-based data storage.

chapters.title: Smart Pointers and Modern Memory Management
chapters.description: Use unique_ptr, shared_ptr, weak_ptr, RAII principle, and avoid memory leaks in modern C++.

chapters.title: Competitive Programming Patterns in C++
chapters.description: Fast I/O, common CP patterns, two pointers, sliding window, prefix sums, and optimization techniques.

chapters.title: Modern C++ Features (C++11 to C++20)
chapters.description: Lambda expressions, auto, range-based for, move semantics, constexpr, structured bindings, and concepts.

courseCta.text: Want to learn C++ with a live mentor?
